chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.D-1038 of 2023 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. FRESH CASE 1.For orders on CMA 4384/2023 2.For orders on office objections. 3.For orders on CMA 4385/2023 4.For orders on CMA 4386/2023 5.For hearing of main case 27-06-2023 Mr. Ghulam Murtaza Korai, Advocate along with petitioner No.1. ***** 1. Urgency is granted. 2-5. Mst. Aneelan (petitioner No.1) is present. She appears to be an adult. Aunt of her husband is also present. Prima facie, it does not appear to be a case of trafficking; however, we cannot divulge into the deeper enquiry at this stage. FIR No.52 of 2023, under Sections 452 & 365-B PPC at P.S, Sarhad-Ghotki has been registered against husband of the petitioner No.1 and his relatives. No arrest is to be made pursuant to FIR No.52 of 2023 for a period of three weeks. During this three weeks’ period, I.O of the case will record statement of this girl Aneelan and her husband Riaz Ahmed and along with statement file the same with supplementary Challan before learned Magistrate, who is seized of the matter. Learned Magistrate shall then apply his independent mind to take a decision on the supplementary Challan which is to be filed. This interim order granting protection to the petitioners shall cease to have effect after expiry of three weeks. It is expected that learned Magistrate shall take decision prior to that and in the event learned Magistrate takes cognizance of the case, sufficient Page 2 of 2 time will be granted to the accused of FIR No.52 of 2023 of P.S, Sarhad to make their relevant bail applications. Learned counsel for the petitioners is satisfied with this order and does not press this petition any further, which stands disposed of in the above terms.

It is expected that learned Magistrate shall take decision prior to that and in the event learned Magistrate takes cognizance of the case, sufficient Page 2 of 2 time will be granted to the accused of FIR No.52 of 2023 of P.S, Sarhad to make their relevant bail applications. Learned counsel for the petitioners is satisfied with this order and does not press this petition any further, which stands disposed of in the above terms. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
